Title: Characterization of four polymorphic genes controlling red leaf colour in lettuce that have undergone disruptive selection since domestication

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: red cultivar S1; green stem lettuce Y37; S9 F2:3 family
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation; loss-of-function allele rll1 causes constitutively green leaves and abolishes stress-inducible red pigmentation in some cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLL1 was mapped as a major qualitative locus controlling red versus green leaf colour. 2. RLL1 encodes a bHLH transcription factor; a 5-bp deletion in exon 7 in the green parent causes a frameshift null allele. 3. Complementation with 35S::RLL1 restored red leaf colour. 4. RLL1 binds DFR and ANS promoters and up-regulates multiple anthocyanin pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DFR and ANS directly; positively up-regulates CHS, DFR, ANS and GST expression overall.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions as the bHLH subunit of the MBW complex; interacts with RLL2 and LsTTG1; also interacts with RLL3.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator; loss of function completely blocks anthocyanin accumulation in leaves in the tested backgrounds.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA+RNA-seq mapping; complementation/overexpression in lettuce; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; metabolite/leaf-colour phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Green cultivars lacking anthocyanins may have decreased ability against biotic and abiotic stresses.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate red pigmentation and anthocyanin content in lettuce breeding; functional allele can be introduced to increase health-promoting flavonoids.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The rll1 loss-of-function mutation was found only in cultivars, not in surveyed wild genotypes, indicating post-domestication selection.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: red cultivar S1; green stem lettuce Y37; Q16 F2:3 family
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation; highly expressed RLL2A from the red parent confers red leaves, whereas low-expression rll2 backgrounds are mostly green.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLL2 was identified as a second major qualitative locus for red versus green leaf colour. 2. The active allele corresponds to RLL2A, an expressed R2R3-MYB copy from the red parent; transformation with RLL2A restored red colour. 3. RLL2 binds DFR and ANS promoters and up-regulates anthocyanin pathway genes. 4. Elevated expression rather than coding divergence was key, and promoter mutation likely activated RLL2 after domestication.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DFR and ANS directly; positively associated with up-regulation of F3H, DFR, ANS and AAC.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MYB component of MBW complex; interacts with RLL1.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator and likely pathway-level activator; elevated expression is sufficient to induce red pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: RLL2A highly expressed; RLL2B and RLL2B-Y37 expressed at low levels.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA+RNA-seq mapping; transgenic overexpression/complementation; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High anthocyanin accumulation in epidermal cells may reduce light penetration, photosynthesis and plant growth.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for increasing anthocyanins; promoter activation or high-expression allele deployment can create red cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RLL2 locus underwent duplication; three homologs (RLL2A/B/C) in S1, but only RLL2A is highly expressed. A 2-bp TA insertion in the promoter is proposed to activate expression.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R3-MYB transcription factor with conserved [D/E]Lx2[R/K]x3Lx6Lx3R motif
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: segregating F2:3 family in RLL1/RLL1 rll2/rll2 background
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of anthocyanin accumulation; loss-of-function/mis-sense allele rll3 increases leaf anthocyanin and causes light red pigmentation in rll2 background.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLL3 was identified as an additional locus modulating anthocyanin accumulation. 2. RLL3 encodes an R3-MYB repressor that does not bind pathway promoters directly. 3. RLL3 interacts with RLL1 and represses anthocyanin biosynthesis by competing with RLL2 for RLL1 binding. 4. The W52S substitution in rll3 abolishes interaction with RLL1 and compromises repression, increasing anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect repression of anthocyanin biosynthetic gene activation by competing with RLL2 for RLL1 binding.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Competes within the MBW regulatory module; interacts with RLL1.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: competitive inhibition of activator complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator that suppresses anthocyanin accumulation; loss of repression promotes red pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA+RNA-seq mapping; yeast two-hybrid; sequence analysis of causal missense variants; leaf-colour phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High anthocyanin accumulation may reduce light penetration and growth.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss-of-function rll3 allele can be used to intensify red colour and anthocyanin content.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two amino acid substitutions were identified (C42Y and W52S), but W52S specifically abolished RLL1 interaction.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40 repeat protein; ortholog of Arabidopsis RUP1/RUP2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses anthocyanin accumulation; missense mutant rll4 attenuates suppression and leads to dark red leaves with high anthocyanin levels.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RLL4 was identified as an additional locus controlling light versus dark red leaves. 2. RLL4 encodes a WD40 regulatory protein orthologous to Arabidopsis RUP1/2, negative regulators of UV-B signalling and anthocyanin biosynthesis. 3. Overexpression of wild-type RLL4 attenuated red pigmentation, whereas mutant rll4 did not. 4. RLL4 down-regulates RLL2 and multiple flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthetic genes, but unlike Arabidopsis RUPs, it does not alter HY5a/HY5b expression in lettuce.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: RLL2; CHS; F3H; DFR; ANS; AAC
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses RLL2 and several anthocyanin biosynthesis genes.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No interaction with RLL2 detected by Y2H; not supported as TTG1 competitor.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulation of pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator whose loss of function strongly increases anthocyanin accumulation and red colour intensity.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Slightly higher transcript level in dark red individuals than in light red individuals, but phenotype is determined by coding mutation rather than expression level.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B discussed; anthocyanin biosynthesis in lettuce is UV-B dependent
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA+RNA-seq mapping; transgenic overexpression of wild-type and mutant alleles; qRT-PCR; phylogenetic analysis; sequence comparison
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing anthocyanins may reduce photosynthesis and growth due to light blocking by epidermal pigments.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: rll4 loss-of-function allele is valuable for breeding deeper red, anthocyanin-rich lettuce cultivars.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Causal coding change reported as V134D in Results; later Discussion mentions V134A, indicating possible inconsistency in the text. RLL4 appears to regulate through a mechanism different from Arabidopsis HY5-dependent RUP action.
